title: Gilgul Neshamot: The Jewish Concept of Reincarnation
title_zh: 灵魂轮回：犹太教的转世观念
description: The kabbalistic teaching that souls return to this world to complete their mission, with sources from the Zohar and the Arizal.
description_zh: 卡巴拉教导认为灵魂回到这个世界来完成其使命，来源于《光辉之书》和阿里扎尔。
description_he: תורת הקבלה שנשמות שבות לעולם הזה כדי להשלים את שליחותן, עם מקורות מהזוהר ומהאריז"ל.
chinese_parallels: Buddhist concept of 轮回 (lúnhuí) compared to Jewish gilgul
Yuan Dynasty (元朝, 1271-1368) corresponds to Zohar composition period
Ming Dynasty (明朝) corresponds to Arizal period
Qing Dynasty (清朝) corresponds to Baal Shem Tov period
Different purposes: Buddhist escape vs Jewish completion
key_sources: Shaar HaGilgulim (The Gate of Reincarnations) by the Arizal
practical_applications: View recurring challenges as soul work spanning lifetimes
Follow passionate pulls toward specific mitzvot
Trust unusual circumstances as divinely chosen opportunities
Focus on present growth rather than past life speculation
